file.content:
class Autocompletable::UsersController < ApplicationController
  def index
    set_page_and_extract_portion_from find_autocompletable_users.with_attached_avatar.ordered, per_page: 20
  end

  private
    def find_autocompletable_users
      params[:query].present? ? users_scope.active.filtered_by(params[:query]) : users_scope.active
    end

    def users_scope
      params[:room_id].present? ? Current.user.rooms.find(params[:room_id]).users : User.all
    end
end

file.content:
require "test_helper"

class Autocompletable::UsersControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    sign_in :david
  end

  test "search returns matching users" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "search results escape HTML in names" do
    users(:david).update!(name: "David <script>alert(123)</script>")

    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David <script>alert(123)</script>", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "room search returns matching users" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:hq).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "room search is scoped by membership" do
    sign_in :kevin

    assert_not_includes users(:kevin).rooms, rooms(:watercooler)

    assert_raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound do
      get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:watercooler).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }
    end
  end
end

content.content:
class User < ApplicationRecord
  include Avatar, Bannable, Bot, Mentionable, Role, Transferable

  has_many :memberships, dependent: :delete_all
  has_many :rooms, through: :memberships

  has_many :reachable_messages, through: :rooms, source: :messages
  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :creator_id

  has_many :push_subscriptions, class_name: "Push::Subscription", dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :boosts, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :booster_id
  has_many :searches, dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :bans, dependent: :destroy

  enum :status, %i[ active deactivated banned ], default: :active

  has_secure_password validations: false

  after_create_commit :grant_membership_to_open_rooms

  scope :ordered, -> { order("LOWER(name)") }
  scope :filtered_by, ->(query) { where("name like ?", "%#{query}%") }

  def initials
    name.scan(/\b\w/).join
  end

  def title
    [ name, bio ].compact_blank.join(" – ")
  end

  def deactivate
    transaction do
      close_remote_connections

      memberships.without_direct_rooms.delete_all
      push_subscriptions.delete_all
      searches.delete_all
      sessions.delete_all

      update! status: :deactivated, email_address: deactived_email_address
    end
  end

  def reset_remote_connections
    close_remote_connections reconnect: true
  end

  private
    def grant_membership_to_open_rooms
      Membership.insert_all(Rooms::Open.pluck(:id).collect { |room_id| { room_id: room_id, user_id: id } })
    end

    def deactived_email_address
      email_address&.gsub(/@/, "-deactivated-#{SecureRandom.uuid}@")
    end

tool_use_result.originalFile:
class User < ApplicationRecord
  include Avatar, Bannable, Bot, Mentionable, Role, Transferable

  has_many :memberships, dependent: :delete_all
  has_many :rooms, through: :memberships

  has_many :reachable_messages, through: :rooms, source: :messages
  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :creator_id

  has_many :push_subscriptions, class_name: "Push::Subscription", dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :boosts, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :booster_id
  has_many :searches, dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :bans, dependent: :destroy

  enum :status, %i[ active deactivated banned ], default: :active

  has_secure_password validations: false

  after_create_commit :grant_membership_to_open_rooms

  scope :ordered, -> { order("LOWER(name)") }
  scope :filtered_by, ->(query) { where("name like ?", "%#{query}%") }

  def initials
    name.scan(/\b\w/).join
  end

  def title
    [ name, bio ].compact_blank.join(" – ")
  end

  def deactivate
    transaction do
      close_remote_connections

      memberships.without_direct_rooms.delete_all
      push_subscriptions.delete_all
      searches.delete_all
      sessions.delete_all

      update! status: :deactivated, email_address: deactived_email_address
    end
  end

  def reset_remote_connections
    close_remote_connections reconnect: true
  end

  private
    def grant_membership_to_open_rooms
      Membership.insert_all(Rooms::Open.pluck(:id).collect { |room_id| { room_id: room_id, user_id: id } })
    end

    def deactived_email_address
      email_address&.gsub(/@/, "-deactivated-#{SecureRandom.uuid}@")
    end

    def close_remote_connections(reconnect: false)
      ActionCable.server.remote_connections.where(current_user: self).disconnect reconnect: reconnect
    end
end

input.new_string:
private
    def find_autocompletable_users
      scope = params[:query].present? ? users_scope.active.filtered_by(params[:query]) : users_scope.active
      params[:except].present? ? scope.excluding(excluded_user_ids) : scope
    end

    def users_scope
      params[:room_id].present? ? Current.user.rooms.find(params[:room_id]).users : User.all
    end

    def excluded_user_ids
      Array(params[:except]).flat_map { |ids| ids.to_s.split(",") }
    end

    # scoping.

        raise ArgumentError, "You must only pass a single or collection of #{model.name} objects to ##{__callee__}."
      end

      spawn.excluding!(records + relations.flat_map(&:ids))
    end

tool_use_result.originalFile:
class User < ApplicationRecord
  include Avatar, Bannable, Bot, Mentionable, Role, Transferable

  has_many :memberships, dependent: :delete_all
  has_many :rooms, through: :memberships

  has_many :reachable_messages, through: :rooms, source: :messages
  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :creator_id

  has_many :push_subscriptions, class_name: "Push::Subscription", dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :boosts, dependent: :destroy, foreign_key: :booster_id
  has_many :searches, dependent: :delete_all

  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :bans, dependent: :destroy

  enum :status, %i[ active deactivated banned ], default: :active

  has_secure_password validations: false

  after_create_commit :grant_membership_to_open_rooms

  scope :ordered, -> { order("LOWER(name)") }
  scope :filtered_by, ->(query) { where("name like ?", "%#{query}%") }
  scope :excluding, ->(ids) { where.not(id: ids) }

  def initials
    name.scan(/\b\w/).join
  end

  def title
    [ name, bio ].compact_blank.join(" – ")
  end

  def deactivate
    transaction do
      close_remote_connections

      memberships.without_direct_rooms.delete_all
      push_subscriptions.delete_all
      searches.delete_all
      sessions.delete_all

      update! status: :deactivated, email_address: deactived_email_address
    end
  end

  def reset_remote_connections
    close_remote_connections reconnect: true
  end

  private
    def grant_membership_to_open_rooms
      Membership.insert_all(Rooms::Open.pluck(:id).collect { |room_id| { room_id: room_id, user_id: id } })
    end

    def deactived_email_address
      email_address&.gsub(/@/, "-deactivated-#{SecureRandom.uuid}@")
    end

    def close_remote_connections(reconnect: false)
      ActionCable.server.remote_connections.where(current_user: self).disconnect reconnect: reconnect
    end
end

tool_use_result.originalFile:
class Autocompletable::UsersController < ApplicationController
  def index
    set_page_and_extract_portion_from find_autocompletable_users.with_attached_avatar.ordered, per_page: 20
  end

  private
    def find_autocompletable_users
      scope = params[:query].present? ? users_scope.active.filtered_by(params[:query]) : users_scope.active
      params[:except].present? ? scope.excluding(excluded_user_ids) : scope
    end

    def users_scope
      params[:room_id].present? ? Current.user.rooms.find(params[:room_id]).users : User.all
    end

    def excluded_user_ids
      Array(params[:except]).flat_map { |ids| ids.to_s.split(",") }
    end
end

content.text: Confirmed — the name collision with Rails' built-in `Relation#excluding` was the cause. Renaming to `excluding_ids` fixed it. Now let me add test coverage for the new `except` parameter.

test "room search is scoped by membership" do
    sign_in :kevin

    assert_not_includes users(:kevin).rooms, rooms(:watercooler)

    assert_raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound do
      get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:watercooler).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }
    end
  end
end
input.new_string:
test "room search is scoped by membership" do
    sign_in :kevin

    assert_not_includes users(:kevin).rooms, rooms(:watercooler)

    assert_raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound do
      get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:watercooler).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }
    end
  end

  test "search excludes user ids given as an array" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: [ users(:david).id ] }

    assert_response :success
    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
  end

  test "search excludes user ids given as a comma-separated list" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: users(:david).id.to_s }

    assert_response :success
    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
  end

  test "room search excludes user ids" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:hq).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: [ users(:david).id ] }

    assert_response :success
    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
  end
end

tool_use_result.originalFile:
require "test_helper"

class Autocompletable::UsersControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    sign_in :david
  end

  test "search returns matching users" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "search results escape HTML in names" do
    users(:david).update!(name: "David <script>alert(123)</script>")

    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David <script>alert(123)</script>", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "room search returns matching users" do
    get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:hq).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }

    assert_response :success
    assert_equal "David", response.parsed_body.first["name"]
  end

  test "room search is scoped by membership" do
    sign_in :kevin

    assert_not_includes users(:kevin).rooms, rooms(:watercooler)

    assert_raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound do
      get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:watercooler).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }
    end
  end
end

content.text: That single failure is a pre-existing environment issue (missing `ffprobe`/`libjxl` system library for video preview generation), unrelated to this change. Everything else passes, including all autocomplete tests.

content.content:
diff --git a/app/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller.rb b/app/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller.rb
index 0141cfb..1cb3aa2 100644
--- a/app/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller.rb
+++ b/app/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller.rb
@@ -5,10 +5,15 @@ class Autocompletable::UsersController < ApplicationController
 
   private
     def find_autocompletable_users
-      params[:query].present? ? users_scope.active.filtered_by(params[:query]) : users_scope.active
+      scope = params[:query].present? ? users_scope.active.filtered_by(params[:query]) : users_scope.active
+      params[:except].present? ? scope.excluding_ids(excluded_user_ids) : scope
     end
 
     def users_scope
       params[:room_id].present? ? Current.user.rooms.find(params[:room_id]).users : User.all
     end
+
+    def excluded_user_ids
+      Array(params[:except]).flat_map { |ids| ids.to_s.split(",") }
+    end
 end
diff --git a/app/models/user.rb b/app/models/user.rb
index 20d05dd..f284f62 100644
--- a/app/models/user.rb
+++ b/app/models/user.rb
@@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ class User < ApplicationRecord
 
   scope :ordered, -> { order("LOWER(name)") }
   scope :filtered_by, ->(query) { where("name like ?", "%#{query}%") }
+  scope :excluding_ids, ->(ids) { where.not(id: ids) }
 
   def initials
     name.scan(/\b\w/).join
diff --git a/test/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller_test.rb b/test/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller_test.rb
index dbd1315..5ceabe9 100644
--- a/test/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller_test.rb
+++ b/test/controllers/autocompletable/users_controller_test.rb
@@ -37,4 +37,25 @@ class Autocompletable::UsersControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
       get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:watercooler).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da" }
     end
   end
+
+  test "search excludes user ids given as an array" do
+    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: [ users(:david).id ] }
+
+    assert_response :success
+    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
+  end
+
+  test "search excludes user ids given as a comma-separated list" do
+    get autocompletable_users_url(format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: users(:david).id.to_s }
+
+    assert_response :success
+    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
+  end
+
+  test "room search excludes user ids" do
+    get autocompletable_users_url(room_id: rooms(:hq).id, format: :json), params: { query: "da", except: [ users(:david).id ] }
+
+    assert_response :success
+    assert_not_includes response.parsed_body.collect { |user| user["name"] }, "David"
+  end
 end

content.text:
Changes are complete and left uncommitted as requested.

**Summary:** Added an `except` parameter to `Autocompletable::UsersController#index` that excludes matching user IDs from autocomplete results, applying to both plain and room-scoped searches. Accepts either an array (`except[]=1&except[]=2`) or a comma-separated string (`except=1,2`). Implemented via a new `User.excluding_ids` scope (named to avoid colliding with Rails' built-in `Relation#excluding`, which caused an odd enum-redefinition error when I initially reused that name). Added 3 controller tests; full test suite passes except one pre-existing, unrelated failure caused by a missing system `libjxl` library for video preview generation.

result:
Changes are complete and left uncommitted as requested.

**Summary:** Added an `except` parameter to `Autocompletable::UsersController#index` that excludes matching user IDs from autocomplete results, applying to both plain and room-scoped searches. Accepts either an array (`except[]=1&except[]=2`) or a comma-separated string (`except=1,2`). Implemented via a new `User.excluding_ids` scope (named to avoid colliding with Rails' built-in `Relation#excluding`, which caused an odd enum-redefinition error when I initially reused that name). Added 3 controller tests; full test suite passes except one pre-existing, unrelated failure caused by a missing system `libjxl` library for video preview generation.
